text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY / BONE MARROW PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient Name: Margaret Chen
Date of Birth: 1977
Sex: Female
Medical Record Number: [REDACTED]
Ordering Physician: Hematology/Oncology / Bone Marrow Transplant Service
Clinical Indication: Post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) day +30 bone marrow evaluation; engraftment and disease response assessment. History of AML, NOS (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H, RUNX1 S291fs, TET2 Q916*, normal karyotype, ELN 2022 Adverse risk). Received 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin) plus midostaurin; achieved morphologic CR with MRD negativity after cycle 1. Proceeded to myeloablative alloHSCT with 10/10 HLA-MUD peripheral blood stem cell graft (male donor, CMV-seropositive) following FluBu4 conditioning with tacrolimus/methotrexate GVHD prophylaxis.

---

SPECIMEN INFORMATION

Specimen ID: [LAB ACCESSION NUMBER REDACTED]
Date of Procedure: [DATE REDACTED - POST-TRANSPLANT DAY +30]
Type of Specimen:
  - Bone marrow core biopsy, right posterior iliac crest (1 core, 1.8 cm in length)
  - Bone marrow aspirate, right posterior iliac crest (multiple passes; adequate particles obtained)
  - Peripheral blood submitted concurrently for chimerism studies

Received: Bone marrow core biopsy in formalin; aspirate smears prepared at bedside and air-dried; clot section prepared from residual aspirate material.

Adequacy: Adequate for evaluation. Core biopsy length 1.8 cm with interpretable marrow spaces across the full length. Aspirate: adequate spicules and cellular trails for differential count (200 nucleated cells counted).

---

CLINICAL HISTORY (as provided by ordering clinician):

Ms. Chen is a 48-year-old female with a history of de novo AML with normal karyotype harboring FLT3-ITD (allelic ratio 0.68, high), DNMT3A R882H, RUNX1 S291fs*16 frameshift, TET2 Q916*, and WT1 R462Q mutations. She was treated with 7+3 induction (cytarabine 100 mg/m2/day x7 + daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 x3) plus midostaurin 50 mg BID (days 8-21). Day +28 post-induction bone marrow confirmed morphologic complete remission (3% blasts) with MRD negativity by flow cytometry. Given adverse ELN 2022 risk, she proceeded directly to myeloablative alloHSCT without consolidation. Conditioning: fludarabine/busulfan (FluBu4). Graft source: 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor peripheral blood stem cells, CD34+ dose 5.2 x 10^6/kg. Day 0 infusion completed without significant immediate complications. Post-transplant course notable for grade 3 mucositis (days +8 through approximately +18), grade 2 nausea/vomiting, transient tacrolimus supratherapeutic level with mild acute kidney injury (resolved). Current bone marrow assessment is for engraftment confirmation and disease response evaluation.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION

Core Biopsy: Received in formalin is a single off-white to tan cylindrical core of bone marrow tissue measuring 1.8 cm in length x 0.15 cm in diameter. The specimen is processed per standard decalcification protocol (formic acid-based). Following decalcification, the core is sectioned longitudinally and entirely embedded in a single paraffin block. Sections cut at 4 microns and stained with hematoxylin and eosin (H&E). Additional sections retained on charged slides for ancillary immunohistochemical staining.

Aspirate: Multiple air-dried smears received and stained with Wright-Giemsa. Clot section prepared from residual aspirate material, fixed in formalin, embedded in paraffin, sectioned at 4 microns, and stained with H&E. Additional stains performed as noted below.

Peripheral Blood Smear: Concurrent peripheral smear reviewed at the time of bone marrow assessment.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION

1. CELLULARITY AND ARCHITECTURE

The core biopsy reveals a normocellular bone marrow with an estimated overall cellularity of 50-60% (age-adjusted: within normal to mildly hypercellular range for a 48-year-old female; notable recovery from expected myeloablative aplasia at day +30 post-transplant). Bony trabeculae appear intact without evidence of necrosis, fibrosis, or architectural distortion. The marrow space is populated by a mixture of hematopoietic cells and adipose tissue in proportions consistent with recovery phase hematopoiesis.

Reticulin stain: Grade 0-1/3 reticulin fibrosis (minimal, no significant increase). No collagen fibrosis identified on Masson trichrome stain.

2. ERYTHROPOIESIS

Erythroid precursors are present in normal to mildly increased numbers, arranged in normoblastic islands. Maturation is orderly and complete, progressing from pronormoblasts through orthochromatic normoblasts. No dyserythropoiesis is identified: no nuclear budding, no multinucleation, no nuclear-cytoplasmic asynchrony beyond that expected in a recovery marrow. Mild megaloblastoid changes are present in a small subset of erythroid precursors, likely attributable to recent methotrexate administration (day +1, +3, +6, +11 GVHD prophylaxis). The erythroid-to-myeloid ratio is approximately 1:2 to 1:3, consistent with early-phase reconstitution.

PAS stain: No block positivity in erythroid precursors.
CD235a (glycophorin A) immunostain: Confirms the presence of erythroid elements at normal-to-increased proportions.

3. GRANULOPOIESIS

Myeloid granulocytic precursors are present and demonstrate orderly maturation from myeloblasts through segmented neutrophils. Left-shifted granulopoiesis is noted, with a proportionate increase in myelocytes and metamyelocytes relative to segmented forms, consistent with regenerating/reconstituting marrow. No dysgranulopoiesis is identified: no pseudo-Pelger-Huet anomaly, no hypogranulation, no abnormal nuclear segmentation. Eosinophil and basophil precursors are present in small numbers.

Blast quantification (aspirate differential, 200 cells counted):
  - Myeloblasts: 8/200 = 4% ... NOTE: See below re: further immunophenotypic characterization.

CORRECTED BLAST COUNT FOLLOWING FLOW CYTOMETRY CORRELATION:
Upon correlation with concurrent flow cytometry (see Ancillary Studies below), the cells initially counted morphologically as myeloblasts include a proportion of hematogones (CD34+/CD19+ B-cell precursors), which can appear blast-like in regenerating marrow. Correcting for hematogones, the true myeloblast count is estimated at <1% of total nucleated cells. Hematogones are a recognized feature of post-transplant marrow regeneration and are not a feature of AML recurrence.

4. MEGAKARYOPOIESIS

Megakaryocytes are present in adequate numbers (approximately 4-6 per 10x field on core biopsy). Morphology is within normal limits with appropriate nuclear lobulation and cytoplasmic platelet budding. No micromegakaryocytes or hypolobated megakaryocytes are identified. The presence of maturing megakaryocytes at day +30 is consistent with engraftment of the donor graft.

5. LYMPHOCYTES AND PLASMA CELLS

Small mature lymphocytes are present in scattered aggregates and as interstitial single cells, constituting a normal proportion of nucleated cells. No large atypical lymphocytes or lymphoid aggregates suspicious for lymphoma or post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD) are identified. Plasma cells are present in small numbers (<1%), with mature morphology. No Russell bodies or Dutcher bodies identified.

CD3 immunostain: T-lymphocytes present in scattered distribution, no atypical clustering.
CD20 immunostain: B-lymphocytes present in small numbers; no aggregates suspicious for PTLD.
CD138 immunostain: Plasma cells <1%, scattered, morphologically mature.

6. ASSESSMENT FOR MYELOBLASTS / AML RESIDUAL DISEASE

No sheets or clusters of myeloblasts are identified on core biopsy sections or aspirate smears. Auer rods are not identified. The rare cells with blast-like morphology, as noted above, are attributable to hematogones based on flow cytometry correlation. There is no morphologic evidence of residual or recurrent acute myeloid leukemia.

7. PERIPHERAL BLOOD SMEAR

Concurrent peripheral smear review: WBC differential with rare circulating immature forms (bands and metamyelocytes), consistent with early engraftment/left shift. No circulating blasts identified. No Auer rods. Normochromic, normocytic red blood cells. Platelet numbers are mildly reduced with occasional large platelets noted (consistent with early platelet recovery). No leukoerythroblastic picture.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES

1. FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (MRD ASSESSMENT)

Method: 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry (MFC), leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP)-based and difference-from-normal (DfN) approaches.
Sample: Bone marrow aspirate, fresh.
Total events acquired: 250,000

Findings:
  - A CD34+/CD19+/TdT+ population consistent with hematogones (normal B-cell precursors) is identified, comprising approximately 2.8% of total events. These cells are CD10+, CD22+, CD38+, CD117 negative, CD33 negative, MPO negative, HLA-DR+. This population shows a normal maturation spectrum and is NOT consistent with AML blasts.
  - A population with the previously defined leukemia-associated immunophenotype (CD34+/CD117+/CD33+/CD13+/MPO+/HLA-DR+/CD7-dim) is NOT detected above the sensitivity threshold of 0.01% of total events.
  - No immunophenotypically aberrant myeloid blast population is identified.
  - No evidence of residual acute myeloid leukemia by flow cytometry.
  - Hematogones account for the morphologically blast-like cells as described above.

Conclusion (Flow Cytometry): MRD NEGATIVE by 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry. Sensitivity: 0.01%. Hematogones present (normal post-transplant finding).

2. CYTOGENETICS / CONVENTIONAL KARYOTYPE

Sample: Bone marrow aspirate, fresh, cultured in unstimulated conditions.
Method: G-banded conventional karyotype.
Metaphases analyzed: 20 of 20 adequate.

Result:
  46,XY [20/20 metaphases analyzed]

Interpretation: Male karyotype detected in all 20 metaphases analyzed. The patient is a 46,XX female; a 46,XY karyotype confirms complete conversion to donor (male) karyotype. No clonal cytogenetic abnormalities identified. Normal male karyotype.

Conclusion (Cytogenetics): Complete cytogenetic donor chimerism confirmed. No cytogenetic evidence of AML recurrence. No clonal cytogenetic abnormalities.

3. SHORT TANDEM REPEAT (STR) CHIMERISM ANALYSIS

Sample: Bone marrow aspirate and concurrent peripheral blood, lineage-specific cell sorting.
Method: PCR-based STR analysis with capillary electrophoresis; lineage-specific chimerism performed on CD3+ (T-lymphocyte) and CD33+ (myeloid) sorted fractions.

Results:
  - Total bone marrow chimerism: 98% donor / 2% recipient
  - CD33+ myeloid fraction: 99% donor / 1% recipient
  - CD3+ T-lymphocyte fraction: 92% donor / 8% recipient

Interpretation: Near-complete donor chimerism confirmed in total marrow and myeloid fractions. Mildly lower T-cell chimerism (92% donor) is within expected range at day +30 post-MUD alloHSCT with tacrolimus/methotrexate GVHD prophylaxis, as residual host T-cells are a recognized early post-transplant finding. Mixed T-cell chimerism at this level does not indicate graft failure or impending relapse in the context of full myeloid donor chimerism. Serial monitoring recommended.

Conclusion (STR Chimerism): Engraftment confirmed with near-complete donor chimerism (98% total; 99% myeloid; 92% T-cell).

4. MOLECULAR STUDIES - STATUS AT TIME OF REPORT FINALIZATION:

FLT3-ITD quantitative PCR (bone marrow aspirate): RESULT PENDING - expected within 3-5 business days.
DNMT3A R882H ddPCR (bone marrow aspirate): RESULT PENDING - expected within 3-5 business days.

Note: Molecular MRD results (FLT3-ITD qPCR and DNMT3A R882H ddPCR) are pending at the time of this report. These results will be reported as an addendum when finalized. The overall morphologic and flow cytometric assessment is not expected to change based on molecular results given the degree of MRD negativity achieved.

---

DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS

1. POST-ALLOGENEIC HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANT BONE MARROW (DAY +30): ENGRAFTED DONOR MARROW WITH TRILINEAGE HEMATOPOIESIS.

2. NO MORPHOLOGIC EVIDENCE OF RESIDUAL OR RECURRENT ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA. Blast count <1% of nucleated cells (corrected for hematogones by flow cytometry). No Auer rods.

3. MRD NEGATIVE by 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry (sensitivity 0.01%). The previously defined leukemia-associated immunophenotype is not detected.

4. COMPLETE DONOR CHIMERISM confirmed by conventional karyotype (46,XY in all 20 metaphases, consistent with male donor karyotype in female recipient) and STR analysis (98% total donor chimerism; 99% CD33+ myeloid donor; 92% CD3+ T-cell donor).

5. HEMATOGONES PRESENT (2.8% of events by flow cytometry): Normal B-cell precursor population, consistent with post-transplant marrow regeneration. Not indicative of AML recurrence.

6. MINIMAL RETICULIN FIBROSIS (Grade 0-1/3): No significant fibrosis.

7. MOLECULAR STUDIES (FLT3-ITD qPCR, DNMT3A R882H ddPCR): PENDING - ADDENDUM TO FOLLOW.

---

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY SUMMARY

  CD34: Rare positive cells, <1%, no clusters or sheets (normal distribution)
  CD117 (c-KIT): Rare mast cells and rare progenitors; no aberrant mast cell aggregates
  MPO: Positive in myeloid lineage cells with orderly maturation pattern
  CD3: Scattered T-lymphocytes, no atypical aggregates
  CD20: Rare B-lymphocytes; no aggregates
  CD138: Rare scattered plasma cells, <1%
  CD235a (Glycophorin A): Erythroid islands present
  TdT: Positive in hematogone population (CD34+/CD19+ B-cell precursors), consistent with normal regeneration

---

ADDENDUM NOTE (ANTICIPATED)

Addendum report to be issued upon finalization of FLT3-ITD quantitative PCR and DNMT3A R882H ddPCR molecular MRD studies. Clinician will be notified directly upon result availability. Results will be incorporated into the permanent medical record.

---

OVERALL ASSESSMENT

The day +30 post-allogeneic SCT bone marrow biopsy demonstrates successful engraftment with near-complete donor chimerism (98% total; 99% myeloid), normocellular marrow with trilineage hematopoiesis, and no morphologic or flow cytometric evidence of residual or recurrent AML. The finding of hematogones is a normal post-transplant regenerative feature and should not be interpreted as recurrent disease. MRD is negative by flow cytometry at a sensitivity of 0.01%. Cytogenetics confirm complete conversion to the 46,XY donor karyotype. Molecular MRD studies remain pending.

---

Signed electronically by:
Attending Hematopathologist
Division of Hematopathology, Department of Pathology
[SIGNATURE AND CREDENTIALS REDACTED]

Reviewed and co-signed by:
Bone Marrow Transplant Attending Physician
Division of Hematology/Oncology and Bone Marrow Transplantation
[SIGNATURE AND CREDENTIALS REDACTED]

Report status: FINAL (Molecular addendum pending)
